steps.title: Find the area of circle K using its radius
steps.content: Circle $K$ has radius $r = 4$ mm. Using the circle area formula, we get $$A_K = \pi(4)^2 = 16\pi \text{ mm}^2$$

steps.title: Add the areas of circles K and L
steps.content: We have the area of circle $K$ as $16\pi$ mm$^2$ and the area of circle $L$ as $100\pi$ mm$^2$ (given). The total area is $$A_{\text{total}} = 16\pi + 100\pi = 116\pi \text{ mm}^2$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
